title: gingered crumbly portuguese pomegranate tart
description: an individual flavorful dessert featuring a crumbly pastry, flambe portuguese pomegranate filling, and a touch of ginger.
instructions: in a medium bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, and salt.
add butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
press the mixture into the bottom and up the sides of 4 individual mini cocottes.
in a separate bowl, whisk together pomegranate juice, honey, cornstarch, and ginger powder until smooth.
add the crystallized ginger to the pomegranate mixture and stir to combine.
pour the pomegranate mixture into the pastry-lined cocottes.
bake for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is set.
while the tarts are baking, prepare a flambé by igniting a small amount of brandy in a saucepan over medium heat.
once the tarts are done, carefully place a mini cocotte on a heatproof surface and gently pour the flambé over the top of the tart.
return the tart to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is caramelized.
remove the tart from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before garnishing with pomegranate seeds and serving.
